title: eBay
tagline: Staff Software Engineer at eBay — marketplace engineering after years leading Android at Kleinanzeigen / Adevinta.
context: eBay’s marketplace spans discovery, listing, trust, and checkout at global scale. Staff-level work builds on deep classifieds and Android craft from Kleinanzeigen and Adevinta.
did: Staff Software Engineer at eBay (since Dec 2024). Continue marketplace product engineering across the eBay ecosystem — following Senior Android work on Spark Design System, accessibility, and verticals at eBay Kleinanzeigen / Adevinta.
impact: Contributing at Staff level to products used by millions, carrying forward Compose design-system and classifieds expertise into eBay.

title: Kleinanzeigen
tagline: Senior Android Engineer at Adevinta / eBay Kleinanzeigen — Spark Design System, accessibility, and verticals.
context: Kleinanzeigen is Germany’s leading classifieds marketplace. Shipping Compose UI, accessibility, and vertical features requires Europe-wide collaboration across Adevinta brands.
did: Senior Android Engineer (Mar 2020 – Dec 2024). Led Compose UI development for the Spark Design System (components, docs, UI tests, showcase for designers & QA). Created an Accessibility Guild. Across Verticals Markets: led mobile design decisions on C2B, introduced native ads with mobile.de, shipped VR tours / maps / video for Real Estate, and built Pro subscription packages.
impact: Accelerated Spark Android components; Pro subscriptions hit yearly targets early and were recognized among Adevinta’s best projects worldwide; stronger accessibility and cross-tribe delivery on Germany’s go-to classifieds app.

title: leboncoin
tagline: Adevinta classifieds family — Spark Design System work shared across Europe’s major marketplaces including leboncoin.
homeBlurb: Adevinta Spark Design System · classifieds family
context: leboncoin is France’s default for local commerce and sits in the same Adevinta classifieds family as Kleinanzeigen. Shared design systems keep quality consistent across brands.
did: Contributed to Adevinta’s Spark Design System Android / Compose work used across European classifieds brands including leboncoin — components, documentation, testing, and designer/QA showcase tooling while based on the Kleinanzeigen engineering organization.
impact: Helped scale a shared Compose design system across Adevinta marketplaces — craft that transferred into later Staff work at eBay.

title: Yaoota
tagline: Flying Elephant Lab — built Shahry installment payments from scratch and scaled Yaoota search to ~250K users.
context: Flying Elephant Lab in Cairo shipped shopping and fintech apps for the local market — price search at scale plus a new installment payment product.
did: Senior Android Engineer at Flying Elephant Lab LLC (Jul 2018 – Feb 2020). Built Shahry, a secure payment-by-installment app from scratch in Kotlin (MVVM, Agile). Introduced Jenkins CI (lint, JUnit, Espresso, Play alpha deploy). Maintained Yaoota’s Java shopping search app to scale.
impact: Shahry shipped for the local market with automated quality gates; Yaoota search reached about a quarter million users.

title: HitchHiker
tagline: Stabilized the travel/shipping app, shipped sockets chat with bots, and rebuilt toward offline-first MVVM.
homeBlurb: Stabilized app · chat bots · 10× shipping deals
context: Travel and shipping marketplace apps lose trust fast when memory leaks, threading bugs, and weak architecture surface under real use.
did: Senior Android Engineer at HitchHiker Inc. (Sep 2017 – Jun 2018). Stabilized the app with LeakCanary and RxJava; tightened MVP separation of concerns. Integrated sockets chat with bot support. Built an offline-first, Dagger2-modularized reactive MVVM stack with Data Binding, Kotlin, tests, and automated deployment. Revamped UX to Material Design best practices.
impact: Community of travelers grew; monthly shipping deals hit a new 10× record — and the codebase moved onto a sustainable offline-first architecture.

title: Zad
tagline: Junior Android across Egyptian startups — Zad Emotion Assistant hit ~250K MAU at 4.7★.
context: Arabic audiences wanted culturally native apps — mood content, radio, widgets — not thin localizations of Western products.
did: Junior Android Developer at various Egyptian startups (Jan 2014 – Jan 2016). Shipped Zad (Emotion Assistant), FocusMusic.fm streaming, a context-aware TODO Little app, an Egyptian FlashWatch widget, and an on-device OpenCV photo-comparison app.
impact: Zad reached ~250K MAU with a 4.7 rating — early proof of shipping culturally native Android products at scale.

title: Misho.day
tagline: A prayer-centered daily planner — structure the day around salah, not the other way around.
context: Generic planners ignore prayer times. Muslims juggle calendars, adhan apps, and notes with no salah-first day view.
did: Designed and engineered the web app end-to-end. Centered the UX on prayer windows, daily focus, and a calm UI; shipped public beta at misho.day.
impact: Live product in the Arabic & Islamic tools suite — salah-first planning people can use today.

title: Tawazun.space
tagline: A calm wellness space for reflection and healthier daily rhythm — without gamified noise.
context: Most wellness apps push engagement loops. People seeking balance need a quieter, intentional surface.
did: Owned concept, visual identity, and full-stack implementation. Shipped the public experience at tawazun.space with a distinctive calm aesthetic.
impact: A live wellness product that reads as intentional rest — not another streak counter.

title: IqrarDayn.com
tagline: Clear digital agreements for interest-free (dayn) lending between people.
context: Informal loans strain relationships when terms stay vague. Muslim communities need documentation that respects Islamic principles.
did: Defined product, UX, and engineering for a focused tool that formalizes dayn agreements — structure without interest-based framing.
impact: A dedicated workflow that reduces ambiguity around debt while staying culturally aligned.

title: Loqmaan.com
tagline: A modern Arabic wisdom library — browse timeless quotes without hunting PDFs and feeds.
homeBlurb: Browse Arabic wisdom in one place
context: Arabic wisdom is scattered across social posts and scans. Readers lack a calm, browsable home.
did: Built product, content architecture, and engineering for a bilingual-friendly wisdom platform at loqmaan.com.
impact: A live library for casual browsing and deeper reading of Arabic wisdom.

title: Arabic Watch
tagline: Traditional Arabic timekeeping as a modern, educational web utility.
context: Arabic temporal systems are rich culturally but rare in everyday digital clocks.
did: Researched the model, designed the interaction, and implemented a focused web experiment on Cloudflare Pages.
impact: A working bridge between heritage timekeeping and modern UI craft.

title: Falah.io
tagline: An Islamic tools suite — one calm surface for utilities that are usually scattered.
context: Prayer tools, references, and helpers live in different apps with inconsistent design.
did: Defining suite architecture, brand, and roadmap; early builds in progress toward falah.io.
impact: In progress — aim is one coherent Islamic tools experience. Coming soon.

title: Basira.io
tagline: Clarity-first insight — help people see patterns and decide with more intention.
context: Insight products drown people in dashboards. Clarity beats chart noise for personal decisions.
did: Early concept, brand direction, and product framing for a clarity-first experience in the tools portfolio.
impact: In progress — Basira is being shaped alongside the wider suite. Coming soon.
